title: Chubbit
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Chubbit.png]] |name=Chubbit |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' |class=[[Lizard/Legends|Lizard]] |subspecies= |races= |height=Small{{CSWECite|I|126|chubbits}} |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Light green]][[Classic Star Wars 10|''Classic Star Wars'' 10]] or yellow[[Dark Times 15|''Dark Times'' 15]] with black or red mottling[[Dark Times 13|''Dark Times'' 13]] |haircolor=Blond, red, black, white[[Dark Times 14|''Dark Times'' 14]] |feathers= |eyecolor=White, green, red |distinctions=*Sensitive [[nervous system]] *Slit [[Eye/Legends|pupils]] |lifespan= |origin=[[Aridus/Legends|Aridus]]''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Chubbini]] }} {{Quote|The Chubbits—Aridus's local desert dwellers—and I have just laid waste to an Imperial outpost, Lord Vader… The little creatures have become amazingly loyal to me. It's rather flattering.|An Imperial actor comments on his impersonation of Ben Kenobi to Darth Vader|}} The '''Chubbits''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of bipedal, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]] indigenous to the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Aridus/Legends|Aridus]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]. The average Chubbit stood shorter than a fully grown [[Human/Legends|Human]]. Their skin was [[Color/Legends|green]] or yellow, although some featured red or black spots. Their [[nervous system]] was easily crippled by ultra-strong frequency waves. On their dry [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the Chubbits made use of indigenous technology such as [[wind-runner]]s, wheeled vehicles that were propelled by large sails. The Chubbits suffered after the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], numerous Chubbits were held as [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] individuals by gangs on the planet [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]] and [[Telerath's second moon|one of its moons]]. The fugitive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Dass Jennir]] freed many of these Chubbits. Meanwhile, Imperial troops on Aridus found valuable natural resources there. However, when [[Mining/Legends|mining]] outfits attempted to extract these raw materials, Aridus's ionized [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] rendered their equipment inoperable. The Empire thus approved construction of the [[Iron Tower]], a massive [[power transformer]] and [[signal amplifier]] to rectify the technological problems; the Empire paid little heed when the tower's strong frequency waves crippled the nervous systems and even [[Death/Legends|killed]] many of the planet's natives. The Chubbits responded by waging a [[Chubbit resistance|guerrilla war]] on the Empire, armed with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], [[T2-B Repulsor Tank/Legends|repulsor tanks]], and [[T4-B heavy tank|crawler tanks]] supplied by sympathetic [[Yutrane-Trackata/Legends|Yutrane-Trackata]] workers and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Other offworlders provided support as well, including the Human [[Wrenga Jixton]] and a [[Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator|man]] who claimed to be the Human [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Kenobi impersonator destroyed the Iron Tower when the devotion shown him by the Chubbits and the Rebel Alliance hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] prompted him to thwart a ploy by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to lure Skywalker into a trap at the Iron Tower. In the aftermath of the structure's fall, Jixton swore his services to Vader in exchange for an Imperial retreat from the Chubbit homeworld, and in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] sought an alliance with the species. The [[Yuuzhan Vong]] later conquered Aridus around [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]] during their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Atmospheric conditions on Aridus make long-range communication impossible…''"''
''"''The Iron Tower is something the Empire built to overcome that, Ben?''"''
''"''It's a gigantic power transformer and signal amplifier, Luke… totally automated. Without it… their hovertrain ore transport system couldn't function. But the ultra-strong frequency of its signals has a devastating effect on the native Chubbits… crippling their nervous systems… in time, fatally! The Iron Tower is the greatest symbol of Imperial tyranny on Aridus! While it operates… the Chubbits are being steadily destroyed!"|An Imperial actor portraying Ben Kenobi provides an accurate assessment of the Empire's doings on Aridus|}} [[File:ChubbitNervousSystem-TheReturnOfBenKenobi.png|thumb|left|Chubbit nervous systems were affected by emissions from the Iron Tower.]] A [[Species/Legends|species]] of small [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]]—the average adult came up to a typical [[Human/Legends|Human's]] waist—Chubbits were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Lizard/Legends|lizards]]. The Chubbit body followed a roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] plan, with a head, torso, two [[Limb/Legends|arms]], and two legs. Each hand featured either five or four digits, including an opposable thumb, while their feet gave the species a digitigrade gait on wide, splayed toes.[[Dark Times 16|''Dark Times'' 16]] While some Chubbits sported a stocky build, others had a lanky frame. Skin [[Color/Legends|coloration]] varied from light green to yellow, with the top of the head often sporting mottling of another color, such as red or black. Each Chubbit possessed a broad, flat head that tapered to a blunt snout in the front. Two [[Nose/Legends|nostrils]] pierced the top surface of the muzzle, and a wide [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] split its anterior surface. This [[Tooth/Legends|toothless]] maw housed a pointed, green tongue and pink gums. The two Chubbit [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were placed far apart on either side of the head. They were colored either white, green, or red and their pupils could appear as either small circles or vertical slits. With some Chubbits, a red [[Ear/Legends|tympanic membrane]] was visible directly behind each eye. While many Chubbits were bald, others sported a tuft of [[Hair/Legends|hair]] at the back of the head, tresses that came in shades of black, red, blond, or white. The Chubbit anatomy included a [[nervous system]] that was susceptible to powerful technological emanations of the sort given off by the [[Iron Tower]], a structure built by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] on the Chubbits' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Aridus/Legends|Aridus]].{{CSWECite|II|116|Iron Tower}} The effects of these invisible forces crippled the Chubbit [[Brain/Legends|neurological]] system and eventually proved [[Death/Legends|fatal]] for many individuals.{{CSWECite|I|41|Aridus}} ==Society and culture== [[File:LukeThreepioWind-runner.png|thumb|right|Chubbits operated large ground vehicles known as wind-runners.]] {{Quote|Shoo! Back! Don't you over-curious twerps know better than to crowd an injured person?''"''
''"''Frrr szzzk!''"''
''"''I understood that, you tiny barbarian! I ''am'' a translator droid!|The protocol droid C-3PO meets the Chubbits|}} The Chubbits inhabited the [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Aridus, a remote world considered a backwater by the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Members of the species traveled the sands on domesticated long-necked lizards known as [[droffi steed]]s, which Chubbit riders controlled via saddles, stirrups, and bridles. Others used [[wind-runner]]s, large, ground vehicles that rolled about on wheels and were propelled by winds caught in their large sails. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Chubbits were at ease with advanced weaponry, including [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] and [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton grenades]], although these arms had to be [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] onto their world. On Aridus, Chubbits often wore loose-fitting clothing, which included [[Cloak/Legends|cloaks]], cowls, baggy trousers, headwraps, and [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. Others wore clothing typical of spacefaring species, including long-sleeved [[shirt]]s, trousers, vests, and boots. Offworld Chubbit regalia was similar, with the addition of loose tunics, [[Belt/Legends|belts]], short pants, and slippers. Chubbits who grew out their hair often bound it into a topknot. Chubbits could be suspicious of outsiders, but they were intensely loyal to those who earned their trust.[[Classic Star Wars 11|''Classic Star Wars'' 11]] Indeed, their appreciation for those who helped them could border on adoration. The Chubbit [[Language/Legends|language]] was known as [[Chubbini]]. The tongue was characterized by the widespread use of ''/S/'' and ''/Z/'' sounds. ==History== ===Early history and Imperial occupation=== {{Quote|Threepio, there's no mistake? You saw him too? Ben Kenobi… alive? Right here on Aridus?''"''
''"''He had to move on in a hurry, Master Luke… Apparently, he's organizing the Chubbits—these local desert-dwellers—to resist the Imperial takeover.|Luke and C-3PO|}} [[File:ChubbitResistance-TheReturnOfBenKenobi.png|thumb|left|Chubbit resistance fighters]] The Chubbits were native to Aridus, a world of deserts and [[Magma/Legends|lava]] flows in the [[Flarestar system]]. The [[Star system/Legends|system]] was first reached by galactic [[Scout/Legends|explorers]] during the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]]; it had become part of the known [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' The Chubbit homeworld eventually became part of the [[Narvath sector/Legends|Narvath sector]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} and it was reachable via the [[Gamor Run/Legends|Gamor Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]. From [[1004 BBY|1004]] to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], as the [[New Sith Wars]] raged, the Chubbit home planet was located in territory controlled by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Centuries later, during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Aridus was again allied with the Republic. [[File:ChubbitsGunrunner-TheReturnOfBenKenobi.png|thumb|right|The Rebel Alliance smuggled guns to the Chubbit resistance.]] The Republic's fall and [[Declaration of a New Order|resurrection]] as the Galactic Empire in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]][[Dark Times 11|''Dark Times'' 11]] brought Aridus to newfound prominence. The Chubbits could only stand by idly as the Empire scouted out valuable [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] resources on Aridus and then sent teams of [[Mining/Legends|miners]] to exploit them. When the offworlders discovered that their equipment—everything from [[Plasma-Jet Mole Miner|mole miners]] to [[Comlink/Legends|comlinks]]—was useless on the world due to the planet's ionized [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], they obtained Imperial approval to construct an enormous structure, dubbed the [[Iron Tower]], to act as a [[signal amplifier]], [[power transformer]] and power source for Imperial [[Hovertrain/Legends|hovertrains]], which transported the [[Ore/Legends|ore]]. Mining started back up, and the Chubbits discovered that the Iron Tower's emissions had a devastating effect on their nervous systems; the neurological effects were so critical that many Chubbits even died. Rather than remain passive victims, the Chubbits formed a [[Chubbit resistance|resistance movement]] to fight the Empire and drive it from Aridus. They struck a deal with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which clandestinely [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] weapons and munitions to the Chubbit freedom fighters. Some of the board members of the Aridus-based company [[Yutrane-Trackata/Legends|Yutrane-Trackata]]{{DB|vehicle|t2brepulsortank|T2-B Repulsor Tank}} secretly supported the Chubbit resistance after taking clandestine payments from the Alliance; while the company made mining gear specifically for Imperial operations on Aridus, it provided [[T2-B Repulsor Tank/Legends|T2-B]] [[Hovertank/Legends|repulsor tanks]] and other matériel to the Chubbit freedom fighters by turning a blind eye when Chubbit forces raided its [[Factory/Legends|factories]].[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] Other outsiders supported the Chubbit cause as well; for instance, the Human [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Wrenga Jixton]], hiding out on Aridus, befriended the natives.{{GalaxyCite|10|Shadow Stalker}} ===The Iron Tower=== {{Quote|Those little Chubbits really love and admire you, Ben… Someday maybe I can win the same kind of respect… with you to teach me.''"''
''"''Er… someday, Luke. But for now… the Iron Tower waits!|The adoration of Luke Skywalker and the Chubbits begins to soften the attitude of the Imperial actor portraying Ben Kenobi|}} [[File:IronTower-TheReturnOfBenKenobi.png|thumb|left|upright|The Empire's Iron Tower caused untold harm to Aridus's Chubbit population.]] In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Chubbit resistance provided a convenient cover story for a ruse by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to trap [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the young Human who had destroyed the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Vader had an [[Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator|actor]] surgically altered to resemble [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], Skywalker's deceased [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. The [[Actor/Legends|actor]] then joined the Chubbit resistance and ingratiated himself with the reptilians. The Chubbits accepted the fake Kenobi as the genuine article, and his exploits with them earned their awe and admiration. When Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] [[Skirmish on Aridus|broke up]] a Rebel arms deal with the Chubbits, Vader's gambit escalated; the actor stepped in, apparently to let the [[Unidentified Rebel gunrunner|Rebel gunrunner]] escape. In reality, the man's escape enabled him to flee back to the Rebel base on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] and to tell Skywalker about his encounter with "Ben Kenobi." Skywalker took the bait and traveled to Aridus. As a band of Chubbit resistance fighters attempted to [[Attack on an Imperial hovertrain|assault]] an Imperial hovertrain, Skywalker and his [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], joined them and threw bags full of [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton charges]] at the target, causing it to crash. Skywalker was injured in the attack, so the Chubbits and the Kenobi impersonator tended to his wounds. The fake Kenobi put Skywalker aboard a Chubbit wind-runner for his safety and then led the Chubbits in a [[Raid on an Imperial outpost|raid]] on an [[Aridus Imperial outpost|Imperial outpost]]—another calculated fraud to gain Skywalker's trust. When Skywalker and his [[Droid/Legends|droid]] rejoined the Chubbit resistance fighters, the actor insisted that the time had come to target the Iron Tower—a task he and Skywalker would accomplish alone. To that end, he ordered the Chubbits to bring C-3PO back to Skywalker's [[Starship/Legends|starship]] lest the droid detect the technological fakery that enabled the actor to approximate the real Kenobi's [[The Force/Legends|Force]] powers. The gambit was designed to bring Skywalker to Vader, who was waiting in the Iron Tower, but the awe shown Kenobi by both Skywalker and the Chubbits caused the actor misgivings about the plan. The Chubbits ignored the actor's orders to stay behind to instead secure an elevated position and [[Battle at the Iron Tower|attack]] the Imperial forces at the tower to provide cover for the two infiltrators. However, the Chubbits failed to spot an incoming Imperial [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] headed for their location. Although the actor urged Skywalker to hurry into the tower, the Rebel's insistence on saving the Chubbits broke the actor's resolve; since the real Kenobi would help the small reptiles, he disabled the speeder and saved the freedom fighters. During the subsequent [[Battle at the Iron Tower|mission]] within the Iron Tower, the actor's betrayal of Vader was complete: to help the Chubbits, he pulled a control lever and disabled the tower's systems. The decision cost the actor his life. ===Later history=== [[File:WrengaJixton.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Wrenga Jixton struck a deal with Darth Vader to get the Empire off Aridus.]] {{Quote|Jix offered a deal: I would keep the Empire away from Aridus, and he would do anything I commanded. Jix is an embarrassment, but he is effective. And he knows that to fail me… or to lie to me… would spell death for the planet Aridus. He's given me his life.|Darth Vader explains his deal with Jixton|}} The ensuing explosion knocked Vader unconscious from his perch within the tower. Wrenga Jixton found the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord's]] body and brought him aboard a starship that enabled Vader to escape. Jixton then used the leverage his act earned him to strike a deal with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]]: the Empire would abandon its operations on Aridus in exchange for Jixton's services in the future. Vader agreed, with the caveat that if Jixton ever failed to live up to his end of the bargain, Vader would rain death upon the desert world. The Chubbits survived Vader's threat;''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'', prologue in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'' takes place 36 years after the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]] won by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. According to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', that race took place in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Dark Lord died and the Galactic Empire fragmented, the Chubbits received a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] delegation from the Empire's successor as galactic hegemon, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. That government sought to enter an alliance with the Chubbits so that the New Republic could protect the species from the remnants of the Empire. The Human diplomat [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], daughter of the deceased Vader, led the delegation, fully aware that the Chubbits were still wary of offworlders due to the turmoil they had experienced under Imperial rule. Vader's other offspring, Luke Skywalker, also accompanied the expedition, and during initial talks, Organa learned that many of the Chubbits still fondly remembered Skywalker's exploits on Aridus. Nevertheless, the New Republic [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] left the delegation to seek out more information about his father on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The Flarestar system had between one and ten million sentient inhabitants circa [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. By [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], the planet had fallen under the control of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] during their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the galaxy; it remained so in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. By [[137 ABY]], Aridus fell within territory controlled by the Sith Lord [[Darth Krayt]] as part of his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]]. ==Chubbits in the galaxy== {{Quote|Listen, you must go. It's time for you to leave.''"''
''"''But where? Where can we go?''"''
''"''Wherever you want—someplace safe. Take those ships. Make new lives for yourselves… far away from here.|Jedi Knight Dass Jennir and an [[Unidentified Nosaurian|enslaved Nosaurian]] discuss the options of the newly freed Nosaurians and Chubbits on a moon of Telerath|}} [[File:TsurrNosauriansChubbits-DarkTimes14.png|thumb|left|Chubbits were enslaved alongside Nosaurians on a moon of Telerath.]] Several years before 19 BBY, a [[Jebble Box war|war]] was fought on a desert planet over possession of a legendary artifact known as the [[Dreypa's Oubliette|Jebble Box]]. Chubbits fought in the war using blaster rifles, and some perished in the conflict. By 19 BBY, several Chubbits toiled as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] on the archipelago of [[Noua]] on the planet [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. The Chubbits were held captive by rival gangs, including a [[Musori's gang|band]] of [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslavers]] and a [[Ken-Kiba's spice runner gang|group]] of [[T'surr (species)|T'surri]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] runners. While the Chagrians used their Chubbit captives to act as retainers and carry their [[Sword/Legends|swords]], the T'surri forced them to perform hard labor such as lifting and carrying goods. [[File:ChubbitandpalDT13.png|thumb|right|upright|The planet Telerath was home to many Chubbits.]] The Human [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Dass Jennir]] discovered the plight of Telerath's Chubbit slaves when he fled to the world to escape Imperial persecution after the declaration of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], making the Jedi enemies of the Empire. He was hired by the Human woman [[Ember Chankeli]] to take out the slavers, but she was secretly allied with the spice-runners, who wanted complete control of the Noua underworld. During Jennir's first encounter with the Chagrian slavers, he feigned interest in joining them. When Jennir met the gang's leader, the Chagrian [[Mors Demanna]], a [[Unidentified Chubbit sword bearer|Chubbit slave]] carried Demanna's sword; [[Unidentified Chubbit bystander|another Chubbit]] later ran to the aid of the Human [[Fisher/Legends|fisherman]] [[Dado]] when Demanna [[Dismemberment/Legends|chopped off]] the boy's hand for daring to think he could join the gang. When Jennir began to suspect that the spice-runners kept slaves as well, he infiltrated [[Unidentified Telerath moon base|one of their camps]] on [[Telerath's second moon]], where he found large numbers of Chubbits and [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurians]] forced into hard labor. The Jedi wreaked havoc on the gang's spice refineries at the base, making the destruction seem like a gang attack. The Chubbit and Nosaurian captives fled in the slavers' ships upon Jennir's urging. Jennir later returned to Demanna's slaver gang and asked to join; the same Chubbit retainer took his sword from him before his meeting with [[Musori]], the gang leader. However, the Musori gang discovered Jennir's machinations and beat him severely. Meanwhile, Ken-Kiba's gang took the offensive and captured and killed all of Musori's gang but Demanna himself. When Ken-Kiba's T'surri gangmembers herded various townspeople and the [[Prostitute/Legends|prostitutes]] who worked for Chankeli into [[Ember's Fire|Chankeli's brothel]]; there enslaved Chubbits continued to serve their T'surri masters. Jennir infiltrated the establishment, killed the T'surri guards, and freed the inmates, including the Chubbits, telling them to flee the town.[[Dark Times 17|''Dark Times'' 17]] ==Behind the scenes== [[File:ChubbitSketch-BlueHarvest0.png|thumb|left|upright|Concept sketch of a Chubbit by Douglas Wheatley]] Writer [[Archie Goodwin]] and artist [[Al Williamson]] created the Chubbits for "[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]," a storyline for their weekly [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic strip]]; the Kenobi story was distributed to newspapers by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] from [[May 17]] to [[July 5]], [[1982]]."[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]" For full-color Sunday strips, Chubbits' skin pigmentation was colored light green and pink on separate occasions. In [[1993]], [[Dark Horse Comics]] colorized, reformatted, and reprinted the strips in [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] form as part of its ''[[Classic Star Wars]]'' series. The colors, provided by [[Steve Buccellato]], standardized the skin pigmentation of the Chubbits as light green. Williamson's Chubbits closely resemble a character named [[Gribbet]], who plays a prominent role in another Goodwin–Williamson serialized story, "[[The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell]]," syndicated from [[February 9]] to [[April 19]], [[1981]]."[[The Bounty Hunter of Ord Mantell]]" However, a poster released with the magazine [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 6|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 6]] in [[1996]] identifies Gribbet as a member of the [[Rybet/Legends|Rybet]] species.[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 6|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 6]], pull-out poster Author [[Ryder Windham]] borrowed elements from Goodwin and Williamson's tale for two of his own ''[[Star Wars]]'' stories: the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Shadow Stalker]]'', first published in [[1997]], and the young adult novel ''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'', first published in [[2009]].''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'' In the former, Windham brought in the Human smuggler Wrenga Jixton, first introduced in the [[1996]] comic [[Shadows of the Empire 2|''Shadows of the Empire'' 2]], written by [[John Wagner]],[[Shadows of the Empire 2|''Shadows of the Empire'' 2]] and explained the fate of the Chubbit's home planet, Aridus, after the events of Goodwin and Williamson's story. In ''The Life of Luke Skywalker'', Windham tied up more loose ends from the comic strip story by framing the bulk of the book—a biography of Luke Skywalker—as a diplomatic visit from the newly formed New Republic to the Chubbits. Nevertheless, the book does not reveal the outcome of the diplomatic talks. In [[2009]], artist [[Douglas Wheatley]] designed a more detailed visual depiction of the species when he included Chubbit characters in the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' comic book for Dark Horse Comics. His version of the species mostly resembles Williamson's, but he added more detailed features, such as wrinkled skin around the lips, more elaborate mottling on the head, skin folds around the eyes, and tympanic membranes behind each eye.[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest 0|''Dark Times: Blue Harvest'' 0]] [[Dark Times 11|''Dark Times'' 11]] features two Chubbits fighting in a war over the Jebble Box. The characters appear in a flashback, and the world is not named; however, its depiction is consistent with that of Aridus, the Chubbit homeworld. The [[2008]] reference work ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' mentions the Chubbits in several entries, but it does not capitalize the word ''chubbit'' in the main entry for the species. However, the entries for the planet Aridus and the Iron Tower do capitalize the species' name. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Chubbits}} *[[Dark Times 11|''Dark Times'' 11]] {{Flash}} *[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 1|''Dark Times: Blue Harvest'', Part 1]] *[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 2|''Dark Times: Blue Harvest'', Part 2]] *[[Dark Times 13|''Dark Times'' 13]] *[[Dark Times 14|''Dark Times'' 14]] *[[Dark Times 15|''Dark Times'' 15]] *[[Dark Times 16|''Dark Times'' 16]] *[[Dark Times 17|''Dark Times'' 17]] *"[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]" {{Reprint|The Return of Ben Kenobi}} {{1st}} *{{GalaxyCite|10|Shadow Stalker}} {{Imo}} *''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'' {{Ab|A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker (audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{InsiderCite|36|The Unseen Planets of Star Wars|The Unseen Planets of ''Star Wars''}} {{Po}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *{{CollectorCite|3|ComicScan|ComicScan: Coming Full Circle}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|vehicle|t2brepulsortank|T2-B Repulsor Tank}} ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Reflist}} }} {{Interlang |de=Chubbit |nl=Chubbit }} [[Category:Expansion Region sentient species]] [[Category:Reptilian sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Chubbits]] }}
